items.title: MNIST digits unfold in 2D
items.scenario: A researcher runs t-SNE on 5,000 MNIST images (784 pixels each), optionally after PCA to 50 dimensions. The 2D plot shows digit groups (0, 1, 6, …) forming separate islands with similar digits nearby—local neighborhood structure from pixel space is visible.
items.explanation: Non-linear manifolds in pixel space become separable regions in 2D; color by digit label confirms local preservation, not that t-SNE was given labels.

items.title: Perplexity changes the neighborhood scale
items.scenario: On 500 Iris samples (4 features, standardized), t-SNE with perplexity=5 produces tight, fragmented micro-clusters; perplexity=30 yields smoother, broader groupings that align better with species when colored.
items.explanation: Perplexity controls how many neighbors each point considers; too low overfits local noise, too high can blur fine structure.

items.title: Word embeddings from NLP
items.scenario: A team embeds 3,000 word vectors (300-D from word2vec) with t-SNE to 2D for a demo. Synonyms and related words (king/queen, Paris/France) appear as local clusters even though global distances between unrelated clusters are not meaningful.
items.explanation: t-SNE is popular for embedding visualization; interpret local proximity, not distance between far-apart word groups.

items.title: Two runs, same data, different global layout
items.scenario: An analyst runs sklearn TSNE twice on the same 1,000-row gene expression matrix with different random_state values. The two plots are rotated/mirrored differently, but neighbors of a chosen point remain largely the same in both maps.
items.explanation: Stochastic optimization fixes local structure more reliably than global orientation; compare runs when perplexity tuning, not a single seed.
